genuine_issues: No reference/ directory — detailed financial-model conventions (color coding, number formatting, documentation requirements for hardcodes) are fully inlined rather than delegated to a sibling doc, making the file long and harder to skim

Heading hierarchy is inconsistent: '## Excel File Workflows' is immediately followed by a '## CRITICAL' sub-topic at the same h2 level, then '### Common Workflow' appears as a child — the intended nesting isn't reflected in the actual heading levels
scripts/recalc.py output format (JSON schema) is documented in two separate places (the workflow section and the verification checklist) with slight differences, creating mild redundancy
llm.best_trait: Triggering description is precise and complete — positive triggers, casual-reference examples, and explicit anti-triggers all in one crisp paragraph

genuine_issues: No anti-trigger note — description never states when NOT to invoke (e.g. simple read-only queries, non-Figma tasks)
Description field uses ALL-CAPS imperatives (MANDATORY, NEVER, MUST) and bold markdown — diverges from house style, which is direct without being shouty
Return-value rules are duplicated between Section 1 (rule 1) and Section 3, adding noise without adding information
Frontmatter description is overlong and doubles as an instruction rather than a clean 2–4 sentence 'what/when' summary
llm.best_trait: Section 9 reference table — precise 'when to load' criteria for each sibling file is exemplary progressive disclosure

genuine_issues: Frontmatter description is a single sentence with no WHEN-to-use context and no anti-trigger note — the house style calls for 2–4 sentences covering what, when, and when not to invoke

No triggering guidance anywhere: a user reading the description cannot tell what prompts ('migrate my Claude config', 'set up Codex') should fire this skill or what shouldn't (e.g. don't invoke if already on Codex-native tooling)

Migration Order section (10 numbered steps, many sub-items) is long enough to deserve its own reference/ file or a collapsed 'quick steps' lead followed by a link — as written it dominates the file and slows orientation

No worked input→output example showing a real source artifact (e.g. a sample .claude/settings.json hook) transformed into a concrete Codex output; the table documents output format but doesn't illustrate a migration
llm.best_trait: Final report table format is exceptionally precise: status values, column semantics, and worked rows leave no ambiguity about expected output

genuine_issues: No anti-trigger clause — description never says when NOT to activate (e.g., general HTML/CSS alt text outside R/Quarto contexts, non-ggplot image libraries)
ARGUMENTS section uses loose prose instead of a structured arg definition block, which is inconsistent with canonical skill style
llm.best_trait: Worked example anchors all three formula parts (code context + caption + good alt text) in a single concrete case
